name: My lawyer used ChatGPT to draft my contract without telling me. Is that okay?
description: No, disclosure and verification required; ask your lawyer and request review.

mainEntity.name: My lawyer used ChatGPT to draft my contract without telling me. Is that okay?

acceptedAnswer.text: No, disclosure and verification required; ask your lawyer and request review.
acceptedAnswer.description: No, lawyers must disclose AI use if it affects work quality and ensure outputs are verified. Context: Ethical rules require transparency and competence; undisclosed AI can breach duty of care. Key factors: ABA Model Rule 1.1 on tech competence, client consent needs, verification mandatory. Practical guidance: Ask your lawyer directly about AI use, request revisions if concerned, consider switching if trust broken. Consult bar ethics hotline.
